answer: Slack Web API requests are authenticated using bearer tokens passed as a parameter named `token` or via an HTTP Authorization header [1][2][3]. Applications typically obtain these tokens through the OAuth 2.0 authorization flow, which involves exchanging a temporary authorization code for an access token using methods like `oauth.v2.access` [4][5]. During development, you may use test tokens [1][2]. For sensitive exchanges, Slack recommends using the HTTP Basic authentication scheme for `client_id` and `client_secret` rather than passing them as request parameters [4][6].

results.title: Slack Web API | Slack Developer Docs
